name: Eggplant Szechuan Style
recipeIngredient: 5 tablespoons Peanut or corn oil, or more if needed
2 teaspoons Ginger, fresh; peeled and grated
1/4 cup Water chestnuts; peeled and chopped (preferably fresh)
1 tablespoon Toasted sesame seeds, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Everyone loves this dish, even those who don't particularly like eggplant. The flavor virtually explodes with a lively combination of sweet, spicy, tart, and savory followed by the refreshing crunch of fresh water chestnuts. Serve as a side dish or first course for Western menus.
2. Cut eggplant into 1/2-inch wide by 2-inch long strips.
3. Mix together soy sauce, sugar, and chicken stock. Set aside.
4. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a wide, flat skillet. When hot, add half of the eggplant. Saute, stirring constantly until seared and wilted, about 5 minutes. Remove to a plate. Cook remaining eggplant, adding more oil if needed. Transfer to plate. Set aside.
5. Heat a wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the remaining tablespoon of oil, ginger, garlic, and chile; cook gently but do not brown. Add water chestnuts and half of the green onions; stir-fry for 5 seconds. Increase heat to high, add reserved soy sauce mixture and the eggplant. Toss quickly over high heat until the sauce is reduced and absorbed into eggplant, 1 to 2 minutes. Fold in vinegar and sesame oil. Remove to a serving dish. Top with remaining green onions and sesame seeds. Serve hot or cold.
